perturbation.original_text: Section 2.03 Compensation to the Contractor. (a) The Contractor's Fees. In consideration of the agreement of the Contractor to provide its services as set forth in this Agreement, the Company will pay the Contractor the following amounts: (i) a monthly service fee of $7.50 per note based on the maximum number of notes outstanding during the month, subject to a monthly minimum of $2,500; (ii) a fee for media services further described in Exhibit A equal to the 15% gross/net differential or the fee equivalent thereof; (iii) a fulfillment fee of $2.00 per investment kit mailed; (iv) a fulfillment fee of $1.00 per address for each bonus offer or marketing postcard mailed, subject to a $10,000 maximum per project.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.03 Compensation to the Contractor. (a) The Contractor's Fees. In consideration of the agreement of the Contractor to provide its services as set forth in this Agreement, the Company will pay the Contractor fees as agreed upon in writing and amended from time to time. (i) a monthly service fee, subject to a monthly minimum; (ii) a fee for media services further described in Exhibit A; (iii) a fulfillment fee per investment kit mailed; (iv) a fulfillment fee per address for each bonus offer or marketing postcard mailed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific compensation amounts, the contract introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction. The changed text references the monthly fee, media service fee, fulfillment fee, however without specifying the amounts. This contradicts with the original intent of the contract to define clear compensation terms. This omission could lead to disputes over payment amounts, creating uncertainty in enforcement, and later contradictions in invoicing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.03 Compensation to the Contractor. (a) The Contractor's Fees. In consideration of the agreement of the Contractor to provide its services as set forth in this Agreement, the Company will pay the Contractor fees as agreed upon in writing and amended from time to time. (i) a monthly service fee, subject to a monthly minimum; (ii) a fee for media services further described in Exhibit A; (iii) a fulfillment fee per investment kit mailed; (iv) a fulfillment fee per address for each bonus offer or marketing postcard mailed. (b) The Contractor's Expenses. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, each party shall bear all of its own expenses. To the extent that the Company agrees herein to pay specified offering-related expenses such as all marketing and advertising costs, the Company will pay or cause to be paid such expenses whether incurred prior or subsequent to the date of this Agreement (c) Payment of Fees. On the first Business Day of each month, or as soon thereafter as practicable, the Contractor shall provide the Company with a written invoice for the previous month's fees and expenses that are payable with respect to Notes issued up to the last day of such month.. Such fees and expenses will be due and payable by the later of the fifteenth (15 ) day of every month or fifteen (15) days after the date such invoice is received.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific dollar amounts for the contractor's fees, creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted section. While both sections reference the same fees (monthly service, media services, fulfillment fees), the changed text omits the actual compensation amounts, which are necessary for the invoice and payment process detailed in subsection (c) of the contradicted text. The removal of these specific amounts directly contradicts the expectation that the 'fees and expenses' on the invoice will be calculated according to a clearly defined rate, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over payment. This omission creates ambiguity and breaks the compatibility needed for clear enforcement, and is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.01 Services for the Notes. The services to be provided to the Company by the Contractor pursuant to and during the term of this Agreement shall include the following: (a) Marketing and Advertising. During the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall develop and execute a direct response marketing strategy for the Notes designed to meet the Company's capital goals in a timely manner, which shall be subject to the prior approval of the Company. The Contractor shall also oversee designing and printing all marketing materials (subject to the prior approval of the Company), in accordance with the Securities Act, including the applicable rules and regulations and any other requirements of the SEC and any other Governmental Rules. The Contractor will provide the Company with media planning, media buying, media production and media placement services related to the Offering. All ad placements and use of all marketing materials shall be subject to the prior written approval of the Company. The Company will pay all marketing and advertising costs related to the offering, including printing, postage, advertising and web site hosting.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.01 Services for the Notes. The services to be provided to the Company by the Contractor pursuant to and during the term of this Agreement shall include the following: (a) Marketing and Advertising. During the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall develop and execute a marketing strategy for the Notes designed to meet the Company's capital goals in a manner agreed upon by the parties, which shall be subject to ongoing discussion and amendment. The Contractor shall also oversee designing and printing all marketing materials, in accordance with the Securities Act, including the applicable rules and regulations and any other requirements of the SEC and any other Governmental Rules. The Contractor will provide the Company with media planning, media buying, media production and media placement services related to the Offering. All ad placements and use of all marketing materials shall be subject to discussion between the parties. The parties will agree upon the payment of marketing and advertising costs related to the offering, including printing, postage, advertising and web site hosting.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "direct response marketing strategy" to simply "marketing strategy" and "prior approval" to "ongoing discussion and amendment," the contract introduces ambiguity. This omission creates a contradiction because it suggests that the specifics of the marketing strategy are not clearly defined or agreed upon upfront. This contradicts the original intent of having a pre-approved strategy, leading to potential disputes over the marketing approach and its effectiveness. Furthermore, there are contradictions in the statement 'The parties will agree upon the payment of marketing and advertising costs related to the offering'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.01 Services for the Notes. The services to be provided to the Company by the Contractor pursuant to and during the term of this Agreement shall include the following: (a) Marketing and Advertising. During the term of this Agreement, the Contractor shall develop and execute a marketing strategy for the Notes designed to meet the Company's capital goals in a manner agreed upon by the parties, which shall be subject to ongoing discussion and amendment. The Contractor shall also oversee designing and printing all marketing materials, in accordance with the Securities Act, including the applicable rules and regulations and any other requirements of the SEC and any other Governmental Rules. The Contractor will provide the Company with media planning, media buying, media production and media placement services related to the Offering. All ad placements and use of all marketing materials shall be subject to discussion between the parties. The parties will agree upon the payment of marketing and advertising costs related to the offering, including printing, postage, advertising and web site hosting. (i) During the term of this Agreement, the Company hereby grants the Contractor a limited license to use the Company's logo, corporate colors, trademarks, trade names, fonts, and other aspects of corporate identity in advertisements and marketing materials related to the Notes and on the Contractor's website, subject to the Company's prior written approval of the specific use of these items in writing in each instance (which shall not be unreasonably withheld). The Contractor will not make use of the Company's logo, corporate colors, trademarks or trade names in any manner that would reasonably be expected to disparage or damage such marks or the reputation of the Company or diminish the Company's goodwill. It is expressly agreed that the Contractor is not acquiring any right, title or interest in the Company's logo, corporate colors, trademarks, trade names or other intellectual property.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for "prior written approval" from the Company regarding the use of its logo, corporate colors, trademarks, etc., and replaces it with a more ambiguous standard of "discussion between the parties" and "agreement upon" payment terms. This directly contradicts the licensing terms outlined in the contradicted text, which explicitly states the Contractor needs prior written approval for each specific use of the Company's branding. The change introduces uncertainty regarding the control the Company has over its own branding and financial obligations and shifts the payment terms from fixed obligations to ones that need to be agreed upon, impacting the enforceability of those obligations. This creates a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 8.02 Termination. The Company or the Contractor may terminate this Agreement at any time in whole or in part as more specifically provided below, and in such case, the Contractor will be paid fees incurred up to the date of such termination plus its expenses accrued as of such date within 30 days of such termination. The Company will have the ability to terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days' prior written notice to the Contractor. The Contractor will have the ability to terminate this Agreement by giving 90 days' prior written notice to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 8.02 Termination. The Company or the Contractor may terminate this Agreement at any time in whole or in part as more specifically provided below. The Company will have the ability to terminate this Agreement. The Contractor will have the ability to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific notice periods and payment terms upon termination, the contract becomes internally inconsistent. The reference to 'as more specifically provided below' is now false because the specific details about notice periods (60 days for the Company, 90 days for the Contractor) and payment obligations are omitted. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, especially regarding the financial implications of termination, and is now creating a in-text contradiction by reffering to something that doesn't exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8.02 Termination. The Company or the Contractor may terminate this Agreement at any time in whole or in part as more specifically provided below. The Company will have the ability to terminate this Agreement. The Contractor will have the ability to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific notice periods (60 days for the Company, 90 days for the Contractor) and payment terms upon termination, which were present in the original text. This omission contradicts the phrase 'as more specifically provided below' since the details referenced are no longer provided in the changed text. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the financial implications of termination, creating a direct conflict and contradiction.
